completed: WORK-0019: startup.sh printf RCE verified false positive; regression guard (PR #22).
WORK-0020: install.py SKILL_ROOT/repo containment on tier projections (PR #23).

WORK-0021: am_i_done.py [verify] executable allow-list. Audit confirmed a REAL vuln (no allow-list on cmd[0]); fixed with a bare-name allow-list (default pytest/mypy/ruff/npm/pnpm/make + [gate] allowed_commands), interpreter/forwarder denylist, shell-metachar rejection, and cwd containment. Two-cycle security review caught 3 bypasses in the first attempt.
decisions: WORK-0021 audit-first outcome = FIX (real vuln), the counterpart to WORK-0019's guard-only false positive. Both recorded under .work/insights/.
Default allow-list = ticket's set incl. make/npm (operator's informed choice; they run repo-committed scripts, accepted).

Key reframing from review: the allow-list is DEFENSE IN DEPTH, not a complete RCE control — pytest(conftest)/make/npm run committed content; the real untrusted-PR control is GitHub fork-approval. Documented in references/tiers.md and the code comment.
cmd[0] must be a bare name (closes abs-path bypass without breaking .venv/node_modules PATH resolution); denylist enforced in two layers.
notes: Phase 1. WORK-0021 done on branch fix/verify-cmd-allowlist (not yet PR'd). The RCE trio (0019/0020/0021) is complete. Next: WORK-0034 (/iterate drives end-to-end through PR-raised) — the flow-unification item; from WORK-0034 onward, /iterate should carry through to PR-raised in one invocation (ADR-025). Accepted residual minors on WORK-0021: unicode-lookalike path separators (caught by shutil.which), denylist non-exhaustiveness (documented), REPO_ROOT not realpath'd at module load (containment check realpaths both sides).
